chief_complaint: A 43-year-old perimenopausal woman presented with multiple recurrent angina episodes. A 49-year-old man presented with complaints of recurrent chest pain for 7 months. A 68-year-old woman was readmitted for recurrent angina.
history: All three patients had a history of refractory recurrent angina and rapid plaque progression due to intraplaque hemorrhage (IPH). Case 1 had two recent interventions for the left anterior descending coronary artery (LAD). Case 2 and Case 3 had mild-to-moderate stenosis in coronary angiographies.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Coronary angiography revealed rapid plaque progression. Optical coherence tomography revealed intraplaque hemorrhage as a possible cause of plaque progression. Electrocardiography showed dynamic ST-T changes.
correct_diagnosis: Rapid plaque progression due to intraplaque hemorrhage
differentials: Rapid plaque progression due to intraplaque hemorrhage
correct_ranking: Rapid plaque progression due to intraplaque hemorrhage
